notes: Room-relay events (chat from other streamers in a multi-streamer room) never trigger alerts - they are for chat display only. Removed the misleading 'from room relay' condition option, which could never match.
Fix: audio files with embedded album art are no longer misdetected as video and converted to a broken .mp4 - they stay classified as audio.
Fix: video alerts were playing silently - audio now routes correctly through the Web Audio graph.
Fix: vertical alerts could look skewed - the overlay now scales alerts to fit the canvas (with clearer browser-source setup guidance).
Fix: the Placement Editor no longer grows off-screen when its title bar is dragged, and its controls are no longer cut off at the bottom.
Safeguard: plagCue now warns (and blocks) when the overlay port is set to the same value as the plagComms port - a conflict that made the OBS browser source show 'Detail Not Found'.
Image alerts: PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, and WebP files are now a first-class media type with a configurable display duration (default 5 seconds).
Auto-detected media type: the Audio/Video dropdown is replaced by a read-only label - type is inferred from the file extension automatically.
Duplicate alert: every alert card has a duplicate button that creates a copy with a new ID and cleared media path, ready to assign a variant clip.
Amber border on alerts with no media assigned, so unfinished alerts are immediately visible.
JSON import and export for alert configurations.
Placement section hidden for audio alerts - position controls are not relevant for audio-only alerts.
Competition and weight options hidden unless another alert shares the exact same platform, event type, and conditions.
Last media directory remembered within the app session.
Unsaved changes indicator replaced with readable Unsaved Changes! label in alert card headers.
Unsaved changes exit guard: closing via tray Quit prompts to save, close without saving, or cancel if any alert forms have unsaved changes.
Tray close behavior: X button minimizes to tray with a notification; tray Quit exits cleanly without triggering the notification.
Built-in updater: checks for new versions on launch and every 60 seconds, with tray notification and one-click install.

Audio quality fix: alert audio now routes through the Web Audio API (AudioContext) instead of HTML audio/video elements, bypassing Chromium CEF noise suppression and AGC that caused crunchy distorted audio in OBS browser sources.

Loudness normalization: every alert's media is analyzed (EBU R128) so all alerts play back at a consistent, matched loudness instead of some being far too quiet. The volume slider's 75% mark is the normalized level - below attenuates, above boosts - with a limiter to prevent clipping. Existing alerts are analyzed automatically in the background on launch.
Alert sorting: each trigger group has a sort button that cycles through default order, name A-Z, name Z-A, and enabled-first.

Kick platform support: chat, subscription, gift sub, follow, and raid events from Kick (via plagComms) can now trigger alerts. Kick appears as its own platform section with test events, debug filtering, and an Active Platforms toggle.
PyInstaller build size reduction: unused imageio transitive dependencies (numpy, Pillow) excluded from the bundle.
Initial beta release - stream alert overlay manager for multi-platform streamers.

Connects to plagComms via WebSocket and fires custom video or audio alerts in OBS browser sources when stream events occur (subs, donations, gifts, raids, TikTok likes, YouTube superchats, channel point redemptions, and more).
Supports Twitch, TikTok, and YouTube Live simultaneously - one unified alert system for multi-platform streams.
Dual-output overlays: independent horizontal (16:9) and vertical (9:16 / TikTok portrait) browser sources, each with their own placement, size, and position settings.
Fixed and random alert placement modes, with a visual drag-and-drop placement editor and random-bounds canvas.
Alert queue system: sequential mode plays alerts one after another; stack mode overlaps them with a configurable gap.
Per-queue max-length spam protection and inter-alert delay.
Weighted random selection and cooldown timers when multiple alerts compete for the same event.
Condition system: fire alerts only when specific fields match (amount, username, tier, reward name, etc.) with numeric, string, boolean, and range operators.

Automatic media compatibility guard: HEVC and other formats that OBS browser sources cannot play are detected on file selection and at startup, with a one-click conversion to H.264 (no user software required).
Built-in Test button per alert to preview exactly what viewers will see without triggering a real event.
Test Events page to inject any event type manually for full end-to-end testing.
Debug page with per-event trigger trace showing which alerts passed, failed, and why.
Runs entirely on your own machine - no cloud account, no subscription, no third-party alert service.
